name: Should I just take workers comp or can I sue the building owner for my fall?

mainEntity.name: Should I just take workers comp or can I sue the building owner for my fall?

acceptedAnswer.text: You typically can't sue your employer, but you may be able to sue a third-party building owner or contractor for negligence, which can provide compensation for pain and suffering.
acceptedAnswer.description: You generally cannot sue your employer directly for a fall due to the "exclusive remedy" rule of workers' compensation. However, if your fall was caused by a third party—such as a cleaning company that left a floor wet without signs, or a contractor who left debris in a walkway—you may have a separate personal injury lawsuit against them. This is called a "third-party claim." Unlike workers' comp, a third-party claim allows you to seek damages for pain, suffering, and emotional distress. This significantly increases the potential recovery. Determining if a third party is involved requires a detailed investigation of the work site and contracts, which is where a specialized law firm is essential.
